key_insight: A ~200 μm Al2O3–TiO2 D-gun coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V by forming a protective tribo-layer under sliding contact.
engineering_relevance: This suggests detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend the service life of Ti-6Al-4V components in aerospace, biomedical, and marine tribological applications.
method: Applied Al2O3–TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using D-gun under controlled conditions.
Tested coated and uncoated samples with pin-on-disc wear experiments per ASTM G99.
Built ANN, KNN, and Random Forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: Wear testing covered 40–100 N load, 100–200 rpm sliding speed, and 20–40 min duration; Random Forest gave the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error.
watch_item: The model and coating performance were validated on a limited experimental parameter range, so broader operating conditions and long-term durability still need confirmation.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
